| Recovering Member | Notify registered owner ASAP; at least one telephone attempt |
| Recovering Member | Prepare ROTATION TOW-OWNER NOTIFICATION (PD571-1210) regardless of phone notification |
| Desk Officer | Release vehicle to owner AFTER cancellation of alarm |
| Desk Officer | Determine if patterns of theft/dumping exist; initiate appropriate action |
| Commanding Officer | Review to ensure alarm cancelled and owner notified |

> **Exam Alert:** If final insurance settlement was received, enter name of insurance company on INVOICE under "Lien Holder or True Owner" and notify the insurance company.

---

## P.G. 218-15: Vehicle Stolen Outside NYC and Recovered Within NYC

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Pages:** 1

### Key Differences from 218-14

- Notify **Inter-City Correspondence Unit** for notification to originating police agency
- An Omniform Complaint Revision will **NOT** be generated
- Forward photocopy of INVOICE to Patrol Borough Pattern Identification Module by next business day

---

## P.G. 218-16: Vehicle Stolen Within NYC and Recovered Outside NYC or by Port Authority Police

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Pages:** 2

### Key Steps

- **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit** transmits message through FINEST to precinct of record
- Desk Officer, Precinct of Record: cancel alarm, notify registered owner, ensure Omniform Complaint Revision prepared
- **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit**: forward cancellation acknowledgement to recovering agency and precinct of record

---

## P.G. 218-18: Photographing Stolen Evidence Vehicles When an Arrest Is Made

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To expeditiously release stolen recovered vehicles where arrest is made and vehicle was NOT used in conjunction with any other crime.

### Key Rule

**THE VEHICLE MAY BE RELEASED TO THE OWNER/REPRESENTATIVE AT THE STATIONHOUSE WITHOUT A RELEASE FROM THE DISTRICT ATTORNEY/CORPORATION COUNSEL** — provided evidence photographs are taken.

### Required Photographs

VIN, registration sticker, license plates, each side of vehicle (including vent windows, door locks, handles), front/back, interior (ignition lock, console, radio, dashboard), motor, and all damage.

> **Exam Alert:** Operable vehicles remain at command for **48 hours** pending owner arrival. If unclaimed → Rotation Tow Program → tow facility for **30 days** → pound.

---

## P.G. 218-19: Invoicing Vehicles/Property as Arrest/Investigatory Evidence or for Forfeiture Proceedings or to Determine True Owner

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Pages:** 4

### Vehicles MUST Be Seized as Arrest Evidence When Arrests Made For:

| Charge | Details |
|--------|---------|
| Larceny of vehicle | Grand Larceny Auto (unless value ≤$100 for auto or ≤$1,000 for motorcycle) |
| Unauthorized use of vehicle | — |
| Criminal possession of stolen property (vehicle) | — |
| Possession of vehicle with altered VIN | — |
| Leaving scene of collision — serious/critical injury | — |
| Assault or homicide — vehicle used as weapon | — |
| Illegal Registration — VTL Section 401(1)(a) | Operating an Unregistered Vehicle |

### Vehicles MAY Be Seized for Forfeiture When Used to Transport:

- Controlled Substances (Article 220, Penal Law)
- Gambling Records (Sections 225.20 and 415, PL)
- Untaxed Cigarettes (State Tax Law/Admin Code)
- Equipment used in promoting pornography (Article 410, PL)
- Equipment used in unauthorized recording of sound (Section 420.05, PL)
- Illegally possessed firearms/rifles/shotguns (Section 265, PL)

> **Exam Alert:** In ALL cases of vehicle seizure (arrest evidence or forfeiture), a **VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM (PD571-1218)** must be completed and served on the arrestee per P.G. 218-48.

---

## P.G. 218-20: Delivery of Vehicle to Property Clerk

**Date Effective:** 07/25/25 | **Pages:** 2

### Key Points

- **Traffic Safety Sergeant** coordinates with Property Clerk Auto Pounds to ensure delivery within **48 hours** of seizure
- Assigned member conducts vehicle inspection using FORMS "Vehicle Inspection" function
- Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT WORKSHEET for missing equipment not on original INVOICE
- Vehicle unable to be driven: notify **Fleet Services Division, Department Tow**

> **Sergeant Focus:** The Traffic Safety Sergeant has direct responsibility for coordinating timely vehicle delivery to Property Clerk Auto Pounds.

---

## P.G. 218-21: Rotation Tow

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 9

### Purpose

To process non-evidence stolen or apparently abandoned vehicles, including motorcycles, recovered within NYC.

### Key Definition

**APPARENTLY ABANDONED VEHICLE** — Unoccupied vehicle abandoned per VTL Section 1224 OR observed under circumstances indicating it may have been stolen but no alarm transmitted.

**DOUBLE TOW** — Additional tow truck needed for unusual recovery (embankment, overturned, wedged). NOT authorized solely because vehicle has no wheels.

> **Exam Alert:** Double Tow is approved by selecting "Yes" in the "Double Tow" caption in FORMS-P when preparing the PCI, and entering the TAX # of the authorizing officer. A vehicle with **no wheels** does NOT automatically qualify for a double tow — a tow should handle that with one truck. *(PG 218-21)*

### VTL 1224 — Abandoned Vehicle Time Chart

| Time Left Unattended | Condition |
|---------------------|-----------|
| More than 6 hours | No plates |
| More than 24 hours | Illegally parked |
| More than 48 hours | Legally parked initially, then becomes illegally parked (e.g., alternate side); 48 hours later it is considered abandoned |
| More than 96 hours | On private property without permission of owner |

> **Exam Alert:** Know these VTL 1224 time thresholds cold — they appear frequently in test scenarios involving whether a vehicle qualifies for Ro-Tow. *(PG 218-21)*

### What is NOT Ro-Tow (Memory Aid)

> **Memory Aid — CHASED FIT DOM:** The situations that disqualify a vehicle from Ro-Tow are remembered as: **C**rime (Dept. Tow), **H**ighway (Authorized or Dept. Tow), **A**rrest made (except photo release), **S**afeguard (Aided/Collision), **E**vidence (Dept. Tow), **D**etermine True Owner — **F**orfeiture, **I**nvestigation, **T**ruck (large) — **D**erelict (Sanitation), **O**wner on scene AND recovered prior to police, **M**oped (limited use motorcycle). *(PG 218-21)*

### Vehicle Is NOT Qualified for Rotation Tow If:

1. Arrest made in conjunction with recovery (except photo-release cases)
2. Required as evidence or held for investigation
3. Used in commission of a crime
4. Subject to forfeiture proceedings
5. Meets derelict guidelines
6. Large truck or limited-use motorcycle (moped)
7. Owner on scene and recovers own vehicle
8. Must be safeguarded due to owner inability to remove (prisoner, aided, collision)

> **Exam Alert:** A DSNY (Sanitation Department) sticker or notification does **NOT** automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The **final determination is made by the UMOS** based on a visual inspection using criteria on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. *(PG 218-21)*

### Desk Officer Responsibilities

- Notify next participating tow operator in rotation **IMMEDIATELY**
- Determine availability **PRIOR TO** furnishing vehicle information
- Tow operators **DO NOT** have option of deciding availability **AFTER** being told vehicle type/condition
- Prepare REPORT OF VIOLATION if tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle
- On limited access highways: **ONLY** franchise tow operator may tow

> **Exam Alert:** If a tow operator refuses an assignment AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, prepare a **Report of Violation (LD 6)** AND forward to the **Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP)**. The next tow operator in sequence is used whenever a tow operator is not available for any reason. *(PG 218-21)*

> **Exam Alert:** Do **NOT** cancel the alarm or notify the owner until the tow operator advises that the vehicle has been removed to the storage facility. *(PG 218-21)*

### Commanding Officer/Designee Responsibilities

- **Daily** inspection of Ro-Tow Log for previous 24 hours (sign in remarks column with corrective action)
- **Daily** inspection of Ro-Tow 30-day file to ensure no vehicle is held more than 30 days
- Complaints involving license violations/overcharges by tow operators: **LD6** forwarded to DCWP
- Complaints of theft/other unlawful acts by tow company: **Complaint Report (61)** forwarded to DCWP (for information only)

> **Exam Alert:** Overcharges by tow operators = **LD6 to DCWP**. Theft or other unlawful acts by tow company = **61 to DCWP** (duplicate, informational only). Know which form applies to which complaint type. *(PG 218-21)*

### Tow Operator Rights

Participating tow operators are permitted to view the **Rotation Towing Logs** of precincts within their assigned zone to verify their rotational sequence.

> **See Also:** PG 218-20 — Delivery of vehicle to Property Clerk (48-hour deadline, Traffic Safety Sergeant responsibility).

### Tow Operator Timelines

| Timeline | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Day 1 | Vehicle towed to operator's facility |
| Day 8–30 | Vehicle may be delivered to Property Clerk Auto Pound (weekdays) |
| Day 30 | Vehicles remaining **MUST BE DELIVERED** to Auto Pound by tow operator |
| After 15 days at Auto Pound | Vehicle/motorcycle disposed of according to law |

> **Exam Alert:** An **owner WILL NOT be notified** nor alarm cancelled until tow operator advises vehicle has been removed to storage facility.

> **Exam Alert:** **Final determination** of Rotation Tow eligibility must be made by a uniformed member based on visual inspection — Sanitation Department notification does NOT confer automatic eligibility.

### Other Agencies Authorized to Use Rotation Tow

- NYC Parks Department
- Seagate PD (60th Precinct)

---

## P.G. 218-22: Processing Boats in Police Custody

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Pages:** 2

### Key Points

- Notify owner (if known) and **Harbor Unit** for removal to storage facility
- Separate INVOICE for evidence/property not part of boat equipment
- Alarm NOT cancelled until boat is at storage facility or receipted by owner
- Boat found on a street: contact **Department tow** — NOT Rotation Tow

---

## P.G. 218-48: Vehicle Seizure at Time of Arrest

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure opportunity for a post-seizure retention hearing for individuals from whom a vehicle is seized.

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Arresting Officer | Prepare INVOICE per P.G. 218-19 |
| 2 | Arresting Officer | Complete VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM (PD571-1218) for "Forfeiture" or "Arrest Evidence" |
| 3 | Arresting Officer | Insert precinct vehicle seizure number from COMMAND VEHICLE SEIZURE INDEX (PD571-091) |
| 5 | Arresting Officer | **Activate Body-Worn Camera** when serving VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM |
| 6 | Arresting Officer | Have defendant/operator sign form (note refusal if applicable) |
| 10 | Desk Officer | Ensure four photocopies distributed: defendant, ADA, invoicing officer, arresting officer |

> **Exam Alert:** Failure to serve VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM may result in the **inability to prosecute forfeiture cases**.

> **Exam Alert:** Vehicles registered to third parties are generally **NOT seized for forfeiture** unless beneficial ownership or specific knowledge by the third party can be proven.

> **Sergeant Focus:** Commanding officers must ensure compliance is part of the command's **monthly self-inspection program** monitored by Quality Assurance Section.

---

# Section 218 — Vendor and Peddler Property

## P.G. 218-39: Seizure, Removal, and Disposition of Property from General and Food Vendors

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To remove property from unlicensed food/general vendors or licensed general vendors peddling in violation of law.

### Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **General Vendor** | Person who peddles/sells/leases goods or services, other than food, at retail in a public area |
| **Food Vendor** | Person who peddles/sells food at retail in any public place |
| **Pushcart** | Wheeled vehicle used by vendor, movable with or without motor, no DMV registration required |
| **Stand** | Movable/portable/collapsible structure used to display or store merchandise |
| **Seizure** | Removal pending forfeiture by City's Law Department — NOT returned without written authorization |
| **Removal** | Confiscation — may be returned under certain circumstances even if OATH summons not adjudicated |
| **Property Clerk Division Seal** | Non-releasable, barcoded, pre-numbered security seal for vendor property bags |

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1a | Member | Serve Civil Summons Returnable to OATH |
| 1b | Member | Place seized property in large plastic bags; twist top closed |
| 1c | Member | Affix Property Clerk Division seal with prongs facing inward |
| 4 | Member | Deliver pushcart, stand, goods to command of occurrence |
| 5 | Member | Do NOT permit vendor to assist in removal or ride in Department vehicle (except arrest) |
| 6 | Member | Return vendor's license after issuing summons |
| 7 | Member | Prepare WORKSHEET marked "Removed/Safeguard" |
| 9 | Member | Notify food rescue organization when DOH employee determines food meets sanitary requirements |

### Desk Officer Responsibilities

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 12 | Examine sealed bag — verify seal number matches WORKSHEET and summons |
| 16 | Notify Property Clerk Division if large amount of property |
| 17 | Notify Department of Sanitation for expeditious disposal of spoiled food |
| 18 | Have OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD report prepared within **24 hours** after perishable food disposed |
| 19 | Mail copy of report to vendor via U.S. Mail |

### Exigent Circumstances

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 23 | Notify vendor to stop selling and begin to move within **one minute** |
| 24 | Inform vendor not to return for at least **two hours** (vendor must be notified of time period) |

> **Exam Alert:** Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement: **one supervisor for five police officers**. Dedicated supervisors present at all property seizures when practical.

> **Exam Alert:** If a sealed bag breaks, a supervisor will **re-bag** using the FORMS "repack" function. Do NOT remove property or the original seal — place entire bag with seal into new bag with new seal.

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO must designate sufficient supervisors to frequently inspect perishable foods so items can be condemned and disposed of when necessary.

---

## P.G. 218-40: General Vendor Distributor Forfeiture Procedure

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To confiscate vehicles of unlicensed general vendor distributors and institute forfeiture proceedings.

### Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Distributor** | Person/organization engaged in sale, consignment, or distribution of goods for sale/resale by a general vendor (excluding owners who personally transport and sell their own goods in compliance with law) |
| **Public Space** | All publicly-owned property between property lines on a street, including parks, plazas, roadways, sidewalks, piers, stadiums, terminals |

### Key Steps

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Serve Civil Summons Returnable to OATH for NYC Admin Code 20-474.1 |
| 2 | Member | Confiscate vehicle and contents for forfeiture |
| 3 | Member | Remove vehicle to precinct; comply with P.G. 218-12 |
| 4 | Member | Prepare WORKSHEET — select "FORFEITURE" |
| 6 | Desk Officer | Have vehicle removed to Property Clerk storage facility |
| 9 | Desk Officer | Prepare PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE (PD260-155) when claimant arrives |
| 10 | Desk Officer | Have claimant sign "Peddler Property — Acknowledgment of Demand" tear-off portion |
| 11 | Desk Officer | Forward documents within **one day** of demand to NYC Law Department |

### Required Vehicle Signage

Distributor vehicles must display a sign with:
- Licensee's name and address
- Business telephone numbers
- Words "General Vending Distributor"
- Department of Consumer and Worker Protection telephone complaint number

> Letters/numerals must be at least **1.5 inches** in height, **0.25 inches** in width, black and white.

---

## P.G. 218-41: Unlicensed Peddler Forfeiture Program

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To institute forfeiture proceedings against property seized from unlicensed street vendors.

### Scope

ONLY property of:
- An **unlicensed** food or general vendor, OR
- A **licensed** general vendor violating restricted streets provision (Admin Code 20-465, subd. 1)

is subject to forfeiture. Confiscate only property on display and any cart, table, or vehicle used to display it.

### Key Steps

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Comply with P.G. 218-39 |
| 2 | Member | Select "FORFEITURE" as Property Category |
| 4 | Desk Officer | Prepare PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE upon claimant arrival |
| 6 | Desk Officer | Forward documents within **one day** to NYC Law Department |
| 9 | Desk Officer | Comply with steps 4–8 for EACH SUBSEQUENT claimant |

> **Exam Alert:** Corporation Counsel is responsible for serving the claimant with the summons and complaint — but documents must be forwarded within **one day** of claimant's appearance.

---

## P.G. 218-42: Obtaining and Returning Property Clerk Division Seals

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To record distribution of Property Clerk Division seals and ensure integrity of returned seals.

### Key Points

- Members below rank of captain on peddler enforcement must carry sufficient seals
- Seals are issued in sets of **20, consecutively numbered**
- Member signs PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEAL DISTRIBUTION CARD (PD521-1411)
- Completed CERTIFICATION OF PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEALS (PD521-061) delivered to immediate supervisor
- Unused/damaged seals are **destroyed** — NOT re-issued
- **Integrity Control Officer** makes random inspections

> **Exam Alert:** Member must notify CO **without delay** when a Property Clerk Division seal is damaged or missing.

---

## P.G. 218-43: Removal and Storage Fees for Licensed Food Vendors

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To collect removal and storage fees for licensed food vendor property.

### Fee Schedule

| Item | Fee |
|------|-----|
| Vehicle, cart, stand removed by Department vehicle | **$65.00** |
| Vehicle, cart, stand NOT removed by Department vehicle | **$20.00** |
| Goods seized with vendor's vehicle/cart/stand | **$10.00** |
| Goods only seized | **$20.00** |
| Storage fee | **$5.00 per day** (or part of day) |

> **Exam Alert:** No storage fee for the day property is **initially delivered** to the command.

> **Exam Alert:** Goods/foods and vehicle/cart/stand stored separately are charged **separate** storage fees.

### Special Rules

- Licensed vendor with valid license: property returned upon demand **without payment of any fee**
- Unlicensed peddler property: NOT returned upon payment — subject to forfeiture per P.G. 218-41
- DO NOT release to owner who alleges property was stolen and refuses to pay
- Fees forwarded to **Audits and Accounts Unit** on next business day

---

## P.G. 218-44: Removal and Storage Charges (Vehicles/Boats)

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To collect and forward fees for vehicles or boats in police custody (abandoned, collision, unoccupied boat found adrift — NOT evidence/impounded vehicles).

### Fee Schedule

| Item | Fee |
|------|-----|
| Towing fee (Department tow/launch) | **$25.00** |
| Storage fee (abandoned or collision vehicle/boat) | **$5.00 per day** (no fee for delivery day) |
| Storage fee (stolen vehicle/boat or boat found adrift) | **$5.00 per day** commencing **3 days** after notice to owner by registered mail |

- Prepare FEE RECEIPT (PD122-017) in numerical sequence
- Forward fees to **Audits and Accounts Unit** each day (excluding weekends/holidays)

> **Exam Alert:** DO NOT release vehicle/boat to an owner who alleges it was stolen and **refuses to pay** charges due.

---

# Section 218 — Key Terms, Timeframes, Fees, and Forms

## Property Categories

| Category | Definition |
|----------|-----------|
| **Arrest Evidence** | Property seized for court presentation |
| **DNA Arrest Evidence** | Property seized for court presentation containing potentially probative DNA evidence |
| **Investigatory** | Property seized for investigation with COMPLAINT REPORT and no ARREST WORKSHEET |
| **DNA Investigatory** | Investigatory property containing potentially probative DNA evidence |
| **Forfeiture** | Property used to facilitate a crime or representing proceeds/substituted proceeds |
| **Decedent's Property** | Personal property of a deceased person |
| **Found Property** | Non-contraband property found by a member or civilian |
| **Peddler's Property** | Items removed from vendors (stands, carts) |
| **Amnesty** | Firearms surrendered via Cash for Guns Program |
| **Safekeeping** | Property to be returned to owner |
| **Determine True Owner** | Property where true owner cannot be determined and no immediate arrest warranted |
| **Photo Release** | Recovered stolen vehicles where arrest made and vehicle not used in other crime |
| **Rotation Tow** | Non-evidence stolen or apparently abandoned vehicles/motorcycles recovered in NYC |

## Firearms Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Firearm** | Includes rifles, shotguns, pistols, revolvers, starter's pistols, zip guns, air/CO2 guns, homemade/modified guns, simulated/toy firearms, ghost guns, 3D-printed firearms, stabilizing brace firearms |
| **Cartridge** | Live ammunition — bullet + shell casing as one unit |
| **Fired Bullet** | Part of cartridge fired through barrel |
| **Shell Casing** | Metal casing remaining in/ejecting from firearm after bullet leaves |

## Vehicle Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Apparently Abandoned Vehicle** | Unoccupied vehicle abandoned per VTL 1224 OR observed under circumstances indicating it may be stolen but no alarm transmitted |
| **Double Tow** | Additional tow truck for unusual recovery (embankment, overturned, wedged) — NOT authorized solely because vehicle has no wheels |

## Other Key Definitions

| Term | Definition |
|------|-----------|
| **Regulated Waste** | Blood/infectious materials in liquid/semi-liquid state; contaminated sharps; pathological wastes |
| **Contraband** | Property whose mere possession is prohibited by law (not property merely suspected stolen) |
| **Numismatic/Sentimental Value** | All gold coins, all U.S. silver coins dated 1964 or prior, extremely old bills |
| **Negotiable Instrument** | Cash equivalents: money orders, traveler's checks, bank checks |
| **Co-Occupant** | Person residing on a permanent basis in same premises with deceased |
| **General Vendor** | Person selling goods other than food at retail in a public area |
| **Food Vendor** | Person selling food at retail in any public place |
| **Distributor** | Person/organization engaged in sale/distribution of goods for resale by a general vendor |
| **Handgun** | Any pistol or revolver listed on a Handgun License |

## Critical Timeframes

| Timeframe | Rule | Source |
|-----------|------|--------|
| **48 hours** | Maximum time vehicle may be safeguarded at command (non-evidence) | 218-12 |
| **48 hours** | Maximum time for delivery of seized vehicle to Property Clerk Auto Pound | 218-20 |
| **30 days** | Tow operator MUST deliver vehicle to Auto Pound | 218-21 |
| **15 days** | After 15 days at Auto Pound, vehicle disposed of according to law | 218-21 |
| **15 days** | Retention deadline for investigatory motor vehicles/boats | 218-55 |
| **1 year** | Retention deadline for investigatory property (non-vehicle) | 218-55 |
| **6 years** | Filed INVOICES maintained (each July, previous year boxed to Property Clerk warehouse) | 218-02 |
| **10 days** | Desk officer must authorize release or denial of investigatory evidence | 218-02 |
| **5 days** | Claimant may request CO/designee review after denial of investigatory evidence | 218-02 |
| **30 days** | Handgun license surrender investigation must be completed | 218-45 |
| **60 days** | Investigation if found property not returned by interstate company/airline/bank | 218-27 |
| **24 hours** | Report prepared after disposal of perishable vendor food | 218-39 |
| **120 hours** | Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit used within this period (or medical discretion) | 218-33 |
| **0100 hours** | Daily deadline for firearms requiring lab analysis to be delivered to patrol borough office | 218-23 |
| **1 minute** | Time for vendor to begin moving in exigent circumstances | 218-39 |
| **2 hours** | Minimum time vendor must stay away from location in exigent circumstances | 218-39 |
| **3 business days** | Property Clerk issues check for federal/state forfeiture upon written request | 218-38 |
| **6 hours** | FORMS outage threshold — under 6 hours: prepare WORKSHEET and wait | 218-57 |

## Fee Schedule

| Fee Type | Amount | Source |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Vendor removal — by Department vehicle | $65.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor removal — not by Department vehicle | $20.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor goods seized with vehicle/cart/stand | $10.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor goods only seized | $20.00 | 218-43 |
| Vendor storage (per day or part) | $5.00 | 218-43 |
| Vehicle/boat towing (Department tow) | $25.00 | 218-44 |
| Vehicle/boat storage (per day or part) | $5.00 | 218-44 |

## Key Forms

| Form | Number | Used For |
|------|--------|----------|
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE | PD521-141 | All invoiced property |
| PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE WORKSHEET | PD521-141A | Draft preparation of INVOICE |
| PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT | PD521-1412 | Transferring property between locations |
| PROPERTY TRANSFER REPORT ACCEPTANCE DOCUMENT | PD521-172 | Receipt upon delivery of transferred property |
| REQUEST FOR LABORATORY EXAMINATION REPORT | PD521-168 | Requesting lab analysis of evidence |
| ON-LINE BOOKING SYSTEM ARREST WORKSHEET | PD244-159 | Arrest processing |
| ACKNOWLEDGMENT OF FOUND PROPERTY | PD542-012 | Receipt for found property delivered on patrol |
| REPORT OF UNCLAIMED PROPERTY | PD542-122 | Reporting found property not claimed |
| WARNING NOTICE — VEHICLE IN POLICE CUSTODY | PD571-1211 | Affixed to windshield of vehicles in custody |
| ROTATION TOW — OWNER NOTIFICATION | PD571-1210 | Notifying owner of rotation tow |
| VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM | PD571-1218 | Served on arrestee when vehicle seized |
| COMMAND VEHICLE SEIZURE INDEX | PD571-091 | Tracking vehicle seizure numbers at command |
| FEE RECEIPT | PD122-017 | Collecting removal/storage fees |
| SEAL FOR DOOR OF D.O.A. PREMISES | PD517-120 | Sealing deceased person's premises |
| IDENTIFICATION TAG | PD317-091 | Receipt for body removed by morgue/undertaker |
| NOTICE TO PERSONS FROM WHOM PROPERTY HAS BEEN REMOVED | PD521-124 | Notice of rights to reclaim property |
| HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE | PD641-123 | Directing surrender of handgun license |
| HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE RESPONSE | PD641-1234A | Documenting investigation results |
| PEDDLER FORFEITURE QUESTIONNAIRE | PD260-155 | Documenting claimant information for forfeiture |
| CONSENT TO SUSPECT EVIDENCE COLLECTION KIT | PD321-031 | Suspect consent for forensic evidence exam |
| CERTIFICATION OF PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEALS | PD521-061 | Tracking peddler enforcement seals |
| PROPERTY CLERK DIVISION SEAL DISTRIBUTION CARD | PD521-1411 | Signing for issued seals |
| REQUEST FOR RETENTION | PD521-169 | Requesting retention of invoiced property |
| REQUEST FOR INVOICE CHANGE OF CATEGORY | PD521-167 | Changing property category |
| OFFICIAL LETTERHEAD | PD158-151 | Various notifications |

## Key Acronyms (from The Key Lessons 21–22)

| Acronym | Full Term | Context |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| **FAS** | Firearms Analysis Section | Section of the Police Lab that analyzes firearms evidence |
| **JFTF** | Joint Firearms Task Force | Contact for emergency firearm trace requests |
| **SECK** | Sexual Offense Evidence Collection Kit | DNA evidence category for lab delivery |
| **PTR** | Property Transfer Report | Report used when transferring property between locations |
| **BATF** | Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives | Contacted for routine or emergency firearm traces |
| **DCWP** | Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection | Receives LD6 complaints about tow operator violations |
| **DSNY** | Dept. of Sanitation New York | Tags vehicles for potential Ro-Tow (but UMOS makes final determination) |
| **EPCS** | Evidence Property Control Specialist | Obtains "Ro Tow" copy of PCI and updates FORMS-P |

## Ro-Tow Disqualifiers — "CHASED FIT DOM"

The mnemonic **CHASED FIT DOM** identifies what is NOT a Rotation Tow situation:

| Letter | Situation | Notes |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| **C** | Crime | Dept. Tow |
| **H** | Highway | Authorized Tow or Dept. Tow |
| **A** | Arrest made | Except photo release |
| **S** | Safeguard | Aided/Collision |
| **E** | Evidence | Dept. Tow |
| **D** | Determine True Owner | — |
| **F** | Forfeiture | — |
| **I** | Investigation | — |
| **T** | Truck (large) | — |
| **D** | Derelict | Sanitation guidelines on inside back cover of Ro-Tow Log |
| **O** | Owner on scene AND recovered prior to police | — |
| **M** | Moped (limited use motorcycle) | — |

## VTL 1224 — Abandoned Vehicle Thresholds

| Time | Condition |
|------|-----------|
| More than 6 hours | No plates |
| More than 24 hours | Illegally parked |
| More than 48 hours | Legally parked initially → becomes abandoned |
| More than 96 hours | On private property without owner permission |

## Four Transparent Lab Bags (218-04)

| Bag | Contents |
|-----|---------|
| 1 — GUNS | Firearms and firearm-related evidence |
| 2 — DRUGS | Controlled substances / marijuana (PCI only — no Request for Lab Exam, no 61, no OLBS, no zFINEST) |
| 3 — DNA | DNA evidence including SECKs |
| 4 — CRIMINALISTICS | Trace evidence (hair, fiber, paint, GSR, etc.) |

## Additional Critical Timeframes (from Lessons 21–22)

| Timeframe | Rule | Source |
|-----------|------|--------|
| **0030** | Desk Officer must notify Patrol Boro that evidence is going to Lab | 218-04 |
| **0100** | Lab delivery must occur on 1st platoon prior to this time | 218-04 |
| **0100** | If boro not called by 0030, Borough Clerical Officer calls command | 218-04 |
| **2 weeks** | Call BATF Regional Crime Gun Center for routine trace (after receipt by FAS) | 218-23 |
| **Day 8–30** | Ro-Tow operator may deliver vehicle to Property Clerk Auto Pound on weekdays | 218-21 |
| **Day 30** | Ro-Tow operator MUST deliver vehicle to Auto Pound | 218-21 |
| **After 15 days at Auto Pound** | Unclaimed vehicles disposed of according to law | 218-21 |

## Key Contact Units

| Unit | When to Contact |
|------|----------------|
| **Auto Crime Unit** (0800–2300) | Questions about vehicle immobilization |
| **Stolen Property Inquiry Unit** | Cancel/transmit alarms; ONLY unit that cancels firearms alarms |
| **Inter-City Correspondence Unit** | Vehicle stolen outside NYC recovered inside NYC |
| **Computer Crimes Squad** (Mon–Fri 0600–2000) | Computer evidence seizure |
| **Office of Chief of Detectives** | Computer evidence (after hours) |
| **Asset Forfeiture Unit** (24/7) | Currency/instruments $1,000+ for forfeiture |
| **Bomb Squad Range — Rodman's Neck** (Mon–Fri 0700–1500) | Fireworks delivery (Mon–Fri 7x3) |
| **Bomb Squad Office** | Fireworks delivery at all other times or if Range personnel unavailable |
| **License Division** | Handgun license surrender issues |
| **Legal Bureau** | Suspect evidence consent refusal; civil enforcement questions |
| **Internal Affairs Bureau Command Center** | Property discrepancies; currency deposit discrepancies |
| **Property Clerk Division** | Large seizures (2,000+ bills); large vendor property |
| **Harbor Unit** | Boat removal to storage facility |
| **Fleet Services Division, Department Tow** | Vehicles unable to be driven |
| **Information Technology Bureau Service Desk** | FORMS Property outages |
| **NYPD OCME Liaison Unit** | Unfounded sexual offense cases |
| **FIU OCME Liaison Unit** | Immediate DNA delivery decisions |
| **NYS Tax Office** | Lottery property recovery |
| **Audits and Accounts Unit** | Fee receipts forwarded daily |
| **Police Lab Evidence Control Supervisor** (24/7) | Unusual property before Lab delivery (large seizures, hypos, etc.) |
| **Joint Firearms Task Force (JFTF)** | Emergency firearm trace requests |
| **BATF Regional Crime Gun Center** | Routine firearm trace (2 weeks after receipt by FAS) |
| **Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP)** | Tow operator violations/overcharges (LD6); tow company theft (61 dup.) |

---

# Section 218 — Review Questions

## Instructions

Select the best answer for each question. Answers are provided after each question.

---

**1.** Under P.G. 218-12, which of the following is NOT an authorized method for immobilizing a vehicle in police custody?

A) Removing rotor or coil wire and attaching to INVOICE printout
B) Disconnecting the vehicle battery
C) Utilizing wheel boots
D) Removing starter relay/fuse or fuel pump relay/fuse

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Disconnecting the vehicle battery**

Members should NOT disconnect the battery due to the potential risk of damaging some vehicles. Contact the Auto Crime Unit (0800–2300) with questions. (P.G. 218-12)

</details>

---

**2.** A vehicle may be safeguarded at the command for how long when not required as evidence or forfeiture?

A) 24 hours
B) 48 hours
C) 72 hours
D) 30 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 48 hours**

After 48 hours, the vehicle must be removed to a Property Clerk storage facility. (P.G. 218-12)

</details>

---

**3.** During an inventory search of an automobile, a locked trunk cannot be forced open UNLESS one of several exceptions applies. If the owner does not consent and no exception exists, what is required?

A) A supervisor's approval
B) A court order from the DA's office
C) A search warrant
D) Notification to the Auto Crime Unit

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) A search warrant**

No consent = no inventory of locked items (absent exigent circumstances). A search warrant is required. (P.G. 218-13)

</details>

---

**4.** Under the Rotation Tow program, when must the tow operator deliver a vehicle to the Property Clerk Auto Pound?

A) Within 15 days
B) Within 24 hours
C) By day 30
D) By day 60

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) By day 30**

Vehicles remaining at the tow facility MUST BE DELIVERED to the Auto Pound by the tow operator by day 30. (P.G. 218-21)

</details>

---

**5.** When serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM on a defendant, the arresting officer must:

A) Have a supervisor present as witness
B) Activate the Body-Worn Camera
C) Obtain written consent from the ADA
D) Have the form notarized

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Activate the Body-Worn Camera**

The arresting officer must activate the BWC when serving the VEHICLE SEIZURE FORM. (P.G. 218-48)

</details>

---

**6.** When marking firearm-related evidence, where should initials be scratched on a shell casing recovered from a crime scene?

A) The exterior of the casing
B) The bottom/base of the casing
C) The inside of the casing
D) Nowhere — shell casings are not individually marked

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) The inside of the casing**

Shell casings are marked on the INSIDE. DO NOT mark the exterior or bottom. (P.G. 218-23)

</details>

---

**7.** Firearms requiring laboratory analysis must be delivered to the patrol borough office by what time daily?

A) 2400 hours
B) 0600 hours
C) 0100 hours
D) 1800 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 0100 hours**

All invoiced firearms requiring Firearms Analysis Section examination must be delivered by 0100 hours daily. Delay requires exigent circumstances AND permission from the precinct CO/duty captain. (P.G. 218-23)

</details>

---

**8.** A PROPERTY CLERK INVOICE being sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed how many line items?

A) 10
B) 15
C) 20
D) 25

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 20**

INVOICES sent or potentially sent to the Police Laboratory will not exceed twenty line items. (P.G. 218-01)

</details>

---

**9.** Which unit is the ONLY unit authorized to cancel alarms for firearms?

A) Auto Crime Unit
B) Recovering command desk officer
C) Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
D) Property Clerk Division

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Stolen Property Inquiry Unit**

Cancellation of firearms alarms will be made ONLY by the Stolen Property Inquiry Unit. The recovering command will NOT cancel. (P.G. 218-23)

</details>

---

**10.** When seizing computer evidence, what should a member do if the computer needs to be powered off?

A) Press the on/off switch
B) Hold the power button for 10 seconds
C) Unplug the computer from the wall outlet
D) Close the laptop lid

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Unplug the computer from the wall outlet**

Do NOT use the on/off switch. Unplug from the wall outlet only. Prior to removing the plug, photograph the monitor screen. (P.G. 218-31)

</details>

---

**11.** Invoiced property categorized as Investigatory (non-vehicle) will be disposed of by the Property Clerk after what period if no retention request is submitted?

A) 6 months
B) 1 year
C) 2 years
D) 15 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 1 year**

The Property Clerk will dispose of investigatory property (non-vehicle) after one year from the date of invoice if no retention request is submitted. Vehicles/boats are disposed of after 15 days. (P.G. 218-55)

</details>

---

**12.** When processing non-evidence currency, the command messenger must be accompanied by whom when delivering the deposit bag to the bank?

A) A detective
B) The invoicing officer
C) Another uniformed member of the service or Evidence/Property Control Specialist
D) The desk officer

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Another uniformed member of the service or Evidence/Property Control Specialist**

Currency is delivered to the bank's night deposit vault by the command messenger accompanied by another uniformed member or Evidence/Property Control Specialist. (P.G. 218-36)

</details>

---

**13.** Under P.G. 218-38, what is the minimum value of currency/negotiable instruments that requires notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit for forfeiture processing?

A) $500
B) $1,000
C) $5,000
D) $10,000

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) $1,000**

Seized U.S. currency and/or negotiable instruments valued at $1,000 or more that are deemed forfeitable require notification to the Asset Forfeiture Unit (24/7). (P.G. 218-38)

</details>

---

**14.** When processing a deceased person's property, what must the patrol supervisor obtain before directing a body search?

A) Permission from the commanding officer
B) Conferral with the Medical Examiner
C) A search warrant
D) Written consent from next of kin

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Conferral with the Medical Examiner**

The patrol supervisor must confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner PRIOR to directing a member to search the body for valuables and identification documents. (P.G. 218-28)

</details>

---

**15.** What is the maximum number of fireworks bags that should be transported in an RMP?

A) 2 bags
B) 4 bags
C) 6 bags
D) 10 bags

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 4 bags**

As a general rule, no more than four bags of fireworks should be transported in an RMP, placed in the rear seat away from heat/spark sources. (P.G. 218-53)

</details>

---

**16.** A handgun license surrender investigation must be completed and forms forwarded to the License Division within how many days?

A) 10 days
B) 15 days
C) 30 days
D) 60 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) 30 days**

The investigation must be completed and all related forms forwarded to the License Division, through channels, within thirty days of date the HANDGUN SURRENDER NOTICE was received at command. (P.G. 218-45)

</details>

---

**17.** Under the vendor enforcement procedures, what is the supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement?

A) One supervisor for three officers
B) One supervisor for five officers
C) One supervisor for eight officers
D) One supervisor for ten officers

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) One supervisor for five officers**

Supervisory span of control for peddler enforcement shall not exceed one supervisor for five police officers. (P.G. 218-39)

</details>

---

**18.** When the desk officer receives a request for the return of investigatory evidence, the release must be authorized or denied within how many days?

A) 5 days
B) 10 days
C) 15 days
D) 30 days

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 10 days**

The desk officer must authorize release no more than ten days after receipt of the request, if property is no longer required as evidence. If denied, claimant may request CO/designee review within 5 days. (P.G. 218-02)

</details>

---

**19.** What is the removal fee when a licensed food vendor's vehicle, cart, or stand is removed by a Department vehicle?

A) $20.00
B) $25.00
C) $50.00
D) $65.00

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**D) $65.00**

The removal fee when property is removed by a Department vehicle is $65.00. If NOT removed by Department vehicle, the fee is $20.00. (P.G. 218-43)

</details>

---

**20.** When FORMS Property becomes inoperable for more than six hours and the problem is isolated to the invoicing command only, what should the desk officer instruct?

A) Use pre-printed serialized INVOICES
B) Contact the Property Clerk Division for manual processing
C) Remove property to an adjoining command and invoice there
D) Wait for the system to be restored

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Remove property to an adjoining command and invoice there**

When the outage exceeds six hours and is isolated to the command, the invoicing member removes property to an adjoining command and invoices in compliance with current procedures. The invoicing command (where the incident occurred) is selected as the invoicing command in FORMS. (P.G. 218-57)

</details>

---

**21.** Under P.G. 218-04, a found revolver with no connection to an arrest or criminal investigation would be:

A) Delivered to the Police Laboratory for analysis
B) Delivered to the Firearms Analysis Section (FAS) for tracing
C) Delivered direct to the Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required)
D) Held at the command until a detective supervisor directs otherwise

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**C) Delivered direct to the Property Clerk Division (no lab analysis required)**

Found **revolvers** not associated with an arrest or criminal investigation do NOT require laboratory analysis and go directly to the Property Clerk. By contrast, found **firearms** (semi-automatic handguns, etc.) DO go to the Lab. (P.G. 218-04)

</details>

---

**22.** Under P.G. 218-04, the Desk Officer must notify the Patrol Boro prior to what time that evidence is going to the Laboratory?

A) 0100 hours
B) 0030 hours
C) 2400 hours
D) 2200 hours

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) 0030 hours**

The Desk Officer notifies the Patrol Boro PRIOR to 0030 that there is evidence going to the Lab for delivery. The actual delivery must then occur on the 1st platoon PRIOR to 0100. If the desk officer fails to call the boro by 0030, the Borough Member/Clerical Officer will call command at 0100. (P.G. 218-04)

</details>

---

**23.** Under P.G. 218-04, which of the following forms is NOT attached to the Drugs transparent bag going to the Police Laboratory?

A) Property Clerk Invoice (PCI)
B) Request for Laboratory Examination
C) zFINEST printout
D) All of the above are NOT attached to the Drugs bag

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**D) All of the above are NOT attached to the Drugs bag**

The Drugs bag does NOT get a Request for Laboratory Examination, does NOT get a 61/Omni printout, and does NOT get a zFINEST printout. The ONLY paperwork attached to the Drugs bag is the PCI. (P.G. 218-04)

</details>

---

**24.** A member fires their service weapon during an officer-involved shooting. The recovered shell casings from the scene should be marked:

A) On the exterior of the casing
B) On the inside of the casing only
C) On the bottom of the casing
D) Not marked — shell casings from officer-involved shootings are never marked

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) On the inside of the casing only**

Discharged shell casings are marked on the INSIDE only — never on the exterior or bottom. This applies to all shell casings regardless of the circumstances. (P.G. 218-23)

</details>

---

**25.** When a member wishes to have a firearm sent to the Lab but does NOT want it traced, what must they write and where?

A) "NO TRACE" in the remarks section of the INVOICE only
B) "DO NOT TRACE" in block letters in the remarks section of BOTH the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam
C) "HOLD FOR TRACE" in the arrest paperwork
D) Nothing — tracing is automatic and cannot be declined

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) "DO NOT TRACE" in block letters in the remarks section of BOTH the PCI AND the Request for Lab Exam**

The phrase "DO NOT TRACE" must be written in block letters in the remarks section of both documents. If just one document is marked, the firearm may still be traced. (P.G. 218-23)

</details>

---

**26.** Officers recover an abandoned vehicle with no plates on a highway. It has been unattended for 7 hours. Under VTL 1224, how should this vehicle be classified?

A) Not abandoned — must be unattended 24 hours
B) Abandoned — unattended more than 6 hours with no plates
C) Derelict — subject to Sanitation disposal
D) Evidence — subject to Dept. Tow only

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Abandoned — unattended more than 6 hours with no plates**

Under VTL 1224, a vehicle left unattended for more than 6 hours with no plates is deemed abandoned. This is the shortest of the four time thresholds. (P.G. 218-21)

</details>

---

**27.** A tow operator, after being told the type and condition of a vehicle, refuses to accept a Rotation Tow assignment. The Desk Officer should:

A) Call the next tow operator without any documentation
B) Prepare a Report of Violation (LD6) and forward to DCWP; then call next operator in sequence
C) Notify the commanding officer and await further instructions
D) Prepare a Complaint Report (61) and forward to DCWP

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Prepare a Report of Violation (LD6) and forward to DCWP; then call next operator in sequence**

When a tow operator refuses AFTER being informed of vehicle type and condition, the Desk Officer prepares a Report of Violation (LD6) and forwards it to the Dept. of Consumer/Worker Protection (DCWP). The next tow operator in sequence is then notified. (P.G. 218-21)

</details>

---

**28.** A complaint is received that a Rotation Tow company stole items from a towed vehicle. The correct form and routing is:

A) LD6 (Report of Violation) forwarded to DCWP
B) Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (duplicate, informational only)
C) LD6 forwarded to Internal Affairs Bureau
D) Complaint Report (61) forwarded directly to the tow company's license bureau

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Complaint Report (61) forwarded to DCWP (duplicate, informational only)**

Theft and other unlawful acts by tow companies are recorded on the Complaint Report (61) and the duplicate is forwarded to DCWP for informational purposes only. Overcharges use LD6. Know the distinction. (P.G. 218-21)

</details>

---

**29.** A patrol supervisor responds to a scene where a person has died alone in their apartment. The supervisor wants to search the body. What must be done FIRST?

A) Request Operations to notify the Public Administrator
B) Confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner
C) Obtain a search warrant from the DA
D) Seal the door with a SEAL FOR DOOR OF DOA PREMISES

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Confer with the Medical Examiner or assistant Medical Examiner**

The patrol supervisor must confer with the ME PRIOR to directing any member to search the body. This is a mandatory first step. (P.G. 218-28)

</details>

---

**30.** When securing fireworks for transport in an RMP, members should:

A) Place bags in the trunk, sealed, with windows closed
B) Place up to 4 bags in the rear seat with all windows opened minimum 2 inches
C) Place bags in the front passenger seat with air conditioning running
D) Call Bomb Squad for escort before any transport of fireworks

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) Place up to 4 bags in the rear seat with all windows opened minimum 2 inches**

Fireworks are transported in the REAR SEAT of the RMP (NOT the trunk). All windows must be opened a minimum of 2 inches. Maximum 4 bags per RMP. For a significant amount, call FDNY through 311 for escort. (P.G. 218-53)

</details>

---

**31.** A Sanitation Department worker places a tag on an abandoned-looking vehicle and notifies NYPD. This automatically qualifies the vehicle for Rotation Tow.

A) True — the DSNY notification triggers the Ro-Tow program
B) False — the UMOS must make the final determination by visual inspection
C) True — but only if the vehicle has been tagged for more than 24 hours
D) False — only the Desk Officer can authorize Ro-Tow after DSNY notification

<details><summary>Answer</summary>

**B) False — the UMOS must make the final determination by visual inspection**

A DSNY sticker or notification does NOT automatically make a vehicle eligible for Ro-Tow. The Desk Officer directs a UMOS to inspect the vehicle and decide if it qualifies based on criteria found on the rear inside cover of the Ro-Tow Log. (P.G. 218-21)

</details>

---

# Section 219 — Department Property

## Source
- **Patrol Guide Section:** 219
- **Source PDF:** public-pguide4.pdf (OCR version)
- **Priority:** Low

## Learning Objectives
- Know the vehicle inspection requirements (daily and weekly) and required equipment
- Understand fuel dispensing card types and delivery supervision procedures
- Know the reporting chain and notifications for lost/stolen Department property, firearms, shields, and ID cards
- Identify rules for IT systems security, portable radios, LPR devices, and mobile digital devices
- Understand the eligibility requirements and restrictions for Restricted Parking Permits
- Know the rules governing OMNY Police Passes, E-Z Pass tags, and radiation detection pagers
- Recognize emergency power outage procedures and fuel tank responsibilities

## Chapter Files

| File | Procedures | Topics |
|------|-----------|--------|
| section-219-vehicles-fleet | 219-01 through 219-10 | Vehicle inspection, maintenance, car wash, tires, tire chains, Department tow, unmarked vehicles |
| section-219-fuel-facilities | 219-11, 219-12, 219-13, 219-28 | Fuel dispensing (card types), fuel/heating oil delivery, emergency power outages |
| section-219-it-communications | 219-14, 219-15, 219-23, 219-24, 219-31, 219-32 | Computer security, portable radios, callboxes, cell phones, LPR devices, mobile digital devices |
| section-219-equipment-supplies | 219-17, 219-18, 219-19, 219-25, 219-26, 219-27, 219-29, 219-30 | Patrol kits (PPE), supply inspection, lockers, E-Z Pass, OMNY passes, radiation pagers, parking permits |
| section-219-loss-theft | 219-20, 219-21 | Loss/theft of Department property, lost/stolen firearm/shield/ID card |
| key-terms | — | Definitions, timeframes, forms, contact units, fuel card colors |
| review-questions | — | 15 multiple-choice practice questions |

## Sergeant Focus Areas
- Vehicle inspection accountability (daily tour checks + weekly Sunday inspections)
- Notification chain for lost/stolen property: Desk Officer → CO/Ops Coordinator → IAB + Stolen Property Inquiry Unit
- Disciplinary consultation with Department Advocate's Office for lost/stolen firearms
- ICO duties: monthly DAS audits (5 members), E-Z Pass monitoring, OMNY pass inspections, parking permit background checks
- LPR alarm response: ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN before law enforcement action
- Emergency power: disconnect non-essential equipment from red-plate receptacles; quarterly room surveys
- Restricted Parking Permit: CO has final authority; 5+ red light or 15+ school speed violations = ineligible

---

# Section 219 — Equipment, Supplies, and Assigned Property

## P.G. 219-17: Patrol Kits (Personal Protective Equipment)

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure patrol kits containing personal protective equipment (PPE) are maintained and available.

### Patrol Kit Contents

| Item |
|------|
| Disposable gowns |
| Eye/mouth shields |
| Heavy duty gloves |
| Shoe covers |
| Antiseptic wipes |
| Biohazard bag |

### Biohazard Waste

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Receptacle | **32-gallon** biohazard receptacle at command |
| Pickup | **Medical Division messenger** picks up regulated infectious waste |

> **Exam Alert:** Regulated infectious waste is picked up by the **Medical Division messenger**, not sanitation or other units. *(P.G. 219-17)*

> **See Also:** P.G. 219-01 — Patrol Kit is required equipment in **Marked RMPs only**.

---

## P.G. 219-18: Inspection of Supplies

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure that supplies delivered to the command are properly inspected.

### Key Points

- Inspect deliveries for proper **quantity and quality**
- **Accept or refuse** based on inspection findings
- Report discrepancies to the supplying unit

---

## P.G. 219-19: Department Locker Inspections

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure the proper inspection of Department-assigned lockers.

### Key Points

- Member **witnesses** their own locker inspection when present
- If member is **not present**, another member witnesses the inspection
- Findings documented as appropriate

> **Exam Alert:** A locker inspection must be **witnessed** — either by the assigned member or, if absent, by **another member**.

---

## P.G. 219-25: E-Z Pass Tags

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To account for the safekeeping and usage of E-Z Pass tags assigned to each command.

### Responsibilities

| Role | Duty |
|------|------|
| CO | Ensure lieutenant/patrol supervisor accounts for all tags |
| CO | Have ICO monitor E-Z Pass usage |
| Lt. Platoon Cmdr/Patrol Supervisor | Inspect all vehicles each Sunday after second platoon; verify tags affixed to center top windshield; record serial numbers on VEHICLE INSPECTION REPORT |
| ICO | Maintain inventory; review usage for compliance; incorporate into monthly self-inspection |

### Mounting Rules

| Vehicle Type | Mounting Method |
|-------------|----------------|
| Standard | **Permanently affixed** with double-sided tape (top center windshield) |
| Vehicles routinely leaving city (e.g., Category I) | Secured with **velcro strips** (removable) |

### Transfer Restrictions

- Commands are **NOT authorized** to transfer E-Z Pass tags between vehicles
- All reassignments approved by CO, Fleet Services Division

### Authorized Toll Crossings (Partial List)

Bronx-Whitestone Bridge, George Washington Bridge, Bayonne Bridge, Goethals Bridge, Henry Hudson Bridge, Holland Tunnel, Queens Midtown Tunnel, Lincoln Tunnel, Throgs Neck Bridge, Outerbridge Crossing, Cross Bay Veterans Memorial Bridge, Verrazano-Narrows Bridge, Hugh L. Carey Tunnel, Robert F. Kennedy Bridge, Marine Parkway-Gil Hodges Memorial Bridge

### NOT Authorized Toll Crossings (Partial List)

New Jersey Turnpike, New York State Thruway, Garden State Parkway, New England Thruway, Governor Mario M. Cuomo (Tappan Zee) Bridge

### Lost/Stolen E-Z Pass

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Notify desk officer |
| 2 | Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT |
| 3 | Notify IAB; enter log number on report |
| 4 | Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit of serial number |
| 5 | CO investigates; forwards report to CO, Fleet Services Division |

> **Exam Alert:** E-Z Pass tags are **NOT authorized** for the NJ Turnpike, NY State Thruway, Garden State Parkway, New England Thruway, or Tappan Zee Bridge. *(P.G. 219-25)*

> **Exam Alert:** Commands may **NOT** transfer E-Z Pass tags between vehicles — Fleet Services Division must approve. *(P.G. 219-25)*

> **See Also:** P.G. 219-10 — Category I vehicles (leave the city regularly) use **velcro strips** for E-Z Pass tags instead of permanent double-sided tape.

---

## P.G. 219-26: NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes

**Date Effective:** 01/06/26 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 3 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To provide for the issuance, control, and strict accountability of NYCT NYPD OMNY Police Passes to all uniformed members.

### Key Facts

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Issued by | Human Resources Division |
| Assigned to | Individual uniformed member (NOT the command) |
| Unique serial number | Each pass has its own number |
| Validity period | Generally **7 years** |
| Renewal | Coordinated by Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit |
| Carry requirement | At all times when shield and ID card required |
| Not valid on | **MTA express buses** |
| Usage monitoring | Each use **electronically recorded** by NYC Transit |

### CO Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Maintain account of all passes |
| Ensure all uniformed members have a valid pass |
| Enter serial number on member's FORCE RECORD |
| Have ICO monitor the program |
| Investigate damaged/defective passes |
| Investigate lost/stolen passes per P.G. 219-20 |

### ICO Responsibilities

| Duty |
|------|
| Enter serial number on FORCE RECORD |
| Inspect passes at roll calls and semi-annual uniform inspections |
| Investigate damaged/defective passes; prepare Typed Letterhead report |
| Hand deliver damaged/defective pass to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit |

### Long-Term Leave

- Member must **surrender** OMNY pass immediately prior to long-term leave (military, child care)
- Pass hand-delivered to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit
- Upon return, report prepared requesting re-issuance

### Lost/Stolen Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Notify desk officer |
| 2 | Desk Officer | Notify CO and operations coordinator |
| 3 | Operations Coordinator | Comply with P.G. 219-20; prepare COMPLAINT REPORT |
| 4 | Operations Coordinator | Notify IAB Command Center; enter log number |
| 5 | Operations Coordinator | Notify Stolen Property Inquiry Unit |
| 6 | Operations Coordinator | Notify **Transit Bureau Wheel** and Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit |

### Separation from Service

- All passes surrendered upon separation (retirement, resignation, dismissal)
- Notation on PROPERTY RECEIPT — DISCONTINUANCE OF SERVICE (PD520-013)
- Pass hand-delivered to Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit

> **Exam Alert:** OMNY passes are NOT valid on **MTA express buses**. All other subway/bus use is permitted. *(P.G. 219-26)*

> **Exam Alert:** Passes are assigned to the **individual member**, NOT the command. Commands may NOT assign or reassign passes. *(P.G. 219-26)*

> **Exam Alert:** Unauthorized person using an OMNY pass can be charged with **theft of service** and **impersonation of a Police Officer**. *(P.G. 219-26)*

> **Prior Test (2017 Lt. Exam):** A MetroCard is **lost or stolen** — the **Ops Coordinator investigates** (per 219-20). A MetroCard is **damaged** — the **ICO investigates** (per 219-26). These are different investigators for different situations. *(P.G. 219-20, 219-26)*

> **See Also:** P.G. 219-20 — Loss or Theft of Department Property: use this procedure for lost/stolen OMNY passes, with additional notifications to Transit Bureau Wheel and Shield, ID and Uniformed Services Unit.

---

## P.G. 219-27: Radiation Detection Pagers — Command-Assigned

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.110 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide for control, accountability, and issuance of radiation detection pagers assigned to commands.

### Key Points

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Assigned by | Counterterrorism Central Resource Division through Citywide Counterterrorism Coordinator |
| Training | **ONLY trained** supervisors/members may use pagers |
| Battery type | **AAA** batteries (obtained from Quartermaster Section) |
| Deployment | Every tour — pager(s) assigned to trained patrol supervisor/member |
| Transfer | Commands NOT authorized to transfer pagers to other commands |
| Inspection | Desk officer inspects/accounts for all pagers at start of each tour |
| Command Log | Serial numbers and operating status recorded |
| If alarm activates | Comply with P.G. 212-102 |
| Lost/stolen | Process per P.G. 219-20; notify Citywide Counterterrorism Office immediately |

### Repair/Replacement

- Desk officer investigates and prepares Typed Letterhead report
- Contact **Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator** for return/replacement instructions

---

## P.G. 219-29: NYPD Restricted Parking Permits

**Date Effective:** 05/25/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.64 | **Pages:** 7

### Definition

**NYPD Restricted Parking Permit (Misc. 23-N)** — Cardboard, approximately 4" × 10", with serial number, command code, vehicle license plate number, and expiration date.

### Eligibility Requirements

| Requirement |
|-------------|
| Valid NYS driver's license |
| Valid NYS vehicle registration |
| Valid insurance policy |
| Vehicle registered to member, blood/marriage relative, or registered NYS domestic partner |
| Owner's residence matches member's Department address of record |

### Ineligible Members/Vehicles

| Ineligible |
|------------|
| Member assigned Category I vehicle |
| Members at One Police Plaza with garage access (except Category II) |
| Members on Military & Extended Leave Desk or vacation/terminal leave pending retirement |
| Members under suspension |
| Auxiliary Police members |
| Vehicle with **5+ red light camera violations** or **15+ school speed camera violations** within 12 months |

### Application Schedule (By Borough)

| Borough | Member Submission Deadline | ICO Delivery Deadline |
|---------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| Manhattan | Last business day, 1st week of September | 2nd week of September |
| Bronx | Last business day, 2nd week of September | 3rd week of September |
| Staten Island/Brooklyn | Last business day, 3rd week of September | 4th week of September |
| Queens | Last business day, 4th week of September | 1st week of October |

### ICO Background Checks

| System | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| ZFINEST RALL | NYS Vehicle Registration by Plate Number |
| ZFINEST DCID | NYS Driver Inquiry (suspensions, revocations, scofflaw) |
| CPR Employee Inquiry Report | Department personnel records |
| DAS Summons History | Search by license plate |
| NYC Dept of Finance website | Outstanding parking/photo enforcement fines |

### Key Restrictions

- Permit may **NOT be photocopied or reproduced** — subject to disciplinary action
- Permit may NOT be **laminated**
- Valid ONLY for the vehicle whose license plate appears on the face
- **Non-transferable** — exclusive use of assigned member
- If vehicle changes, notify CO within **30 days**; may use substitute vehicle for **max 30 days**

### Parking Still Prohibited (With or Without Permit)

Double parking, fire hydrant/zone, bus stop/lane, sidewalk, crosswalks/driveways, obstructing traffic, no standing zones, snow emergency, no parking/taxi stand, bicycle lane, no permit zone

> **Exam Alert:** Vehicle with **5+ red light camera** or **15+ school speed camera** violations in 12 months = **ineligible**.

> **Exam Alert:** Restricted Parking Permits may NOT be **photocopied, reproduced, or laminated**.

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO has **final authority** to approve or deny all Restricted Parking Permit applications.

---

## P.G. 219-30: Radiation Detection Pagers — Personally Assigned

**Date Effective:** 11/18/21 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.110 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide for control, accountability, and issuance of radiation detection pagers permanently assigned to individual uniformed members.

### Key Differences from Command-Assigned (219-27)

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Assigned by | Counterterrorism Central Resource Division after training completion |
| Assignment | **Permanently** to individual member (not the command) |
| Documentation | PROPERTY TRANSFER RECEIPT (PD521-145) — copy to CO and ICO |
| Serial number | Listed on member's FORCE RECORD |
| Transfer | Commands **NOT authorized** to transfer between members |
| Carry requirement | At **all times** when performing duty |
| Separation | Surrender to desk officer for return to Patrol Borough Counterterrorism Coordinator |

> **Exam Alert:** Personally assigned radiation pagers must be carried at **all times** while performing duty and are listed on the member's **FORCE RECORD**.

---

# Section 219 — Fuel, Heating, and Facility Operations

## P.G. 219-11: Computerized Fuel Dispensing

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To provide for the proper use of the computerized fuel dispensing system.

### Vehicle Identification Box (VIB)

The VIB is the in-vehicle transponder for automated fuel dispensing. Fuel is dispensed using card-based authorization.

### Card Types

| Card | Color | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| Master | **Green** | Administrative control |
| CRALO | **Yellow** | Command Radio Auto Liaison Officer |
| Non-Fleet | **Orange** | Non-fleet vehicles |
| Private | **Red** | Private vehicles authorized for official use |
| Operator | **White** | Individual operator cards |

### Electric Vehicles

- **ChargePoint cards** issued for electric vehicle charging
- **Strictly prohibited** to charge personal vehicles using Department ChargePoint cards or stations

> **Exam Alert:** It is **strictly prohibited** to charge personal vehicles at Department charging stations.

> **Exam Alert:** Know the fuel card colors: Green = Master, Yellow = CRALO, Orange = Non-Fleet, Red = Private, White = Operator.

---

## P.G. 219-12: Fuel Delivery Supervision

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To ensure proper supervision of fuel deliveries to Department facilities.

### Key Points

| Item | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Tank fill level | **90% ullage amount** (maximum fill capacity) |
| Monitoring | Electronic tank monitor system |
| Supervision | Delivery must be supervised by designated member |

> **Exam Alert:** Fuel tanks are filled to **90% ullage** — never completely full.

---

## P.G. 219-13: Heating Oil Delivery

**Date Effective:** 05/20/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 31 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure proper procedures for receiving heating oil deliveries.

### Key Points

- **DCAS inspector** must sign the vendor delivery ticket **before** accepting delivery
- Do not accept delivery without DCAS inspector signature

> **Exam Alert:** A **DCAS inspector** must sign the vendor delivery ticket before heating oil delivery is accepted.

---

## P.G. 219-28: Emergency Power Outages

**Date Effective:** 01/18/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.4 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To address emergency power outages and ensure non-essential equipment is removed from emergency electrical receptacles.

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Desk Officer/Supervisory Officer | **Immediately** notify Building Maintenance Section (24/7) |
| 1a | Same | Record notification in Telephone Record |
| 2 | Same (if generator activated) | Survey command — ensure only **essential emergency equipment and critical computer systems** operate on emergency power |
| 3 | Same | Have all **non-essential equipment disconnected** from emergency electrical receptacles |

### Non-Essential Equipment (Disconnect Immediately)

- Air conditioners
- Vending machines
- Refrigerators
- Microwaves

### Emergency Receptacle Identification

- **Red cover plate** over the receptacle, OR
- **Raised lettering** on the outlet receptacle

### Fuel Tank Responsibilities

| Configuration | Responsibility |
|--------------|----------------|
| **Single fuel tank** (heating + generator) | Command maintains fuel level **above 50%** |
| **Two fuel tanks** (separate) | Command maintains heating tank; Building Maintenance Section maintains generator tank |

### CO Quarterly Survey

- Commanding officers conduct **quarterly survey** to ensure generator rooms, mechanical rooms, and electrical rooms are used **only for their intended purpose** — not as locker rooms, storage rooms, etc.
- Items in these rooms may create a **fire hazard**

> **Exam Alert:** Commands with a **single fuel tank** must maintain the level **above 50%**.

> **Exam Alert:** Only qualified **Building Maintenance Section** personnel should start or repair the emergency generator. No outside agencies without BMS supervisor approval (absent exigent circumstances).

> **Sergeant Focus:** CO must conduct a **quarterly survey** of generator/mechanical/electrical rooms to ensure proper use only.

---

# Section 219 — Information Technology and Communications Equipment

## P.G. 219-14: Computer Systems — Security and Integrity

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 3

### Purpose

To establish security and integrity procedures for Department computer systems.

### Member of the Service — Responsibilities

- Sign on using confidential password; always sign off even if leaving for **brief time**
- **Strictly** responsible for entries made while signed on
- If password compromised/misused: notify D.O./designated supervisor **OR** ICO (comply with 207-21 Corruption)
- Make only official inquiries relating to Department business
- Do not divulge official business EXCEPT for valid law enforcement purpose (comply with 304-15 Confidentiality)

### Desk Officer/Designated Supervisor — Responsibilities

- Inspect all computer areas of stationhouse at least **once a tour**
- Record results of inspection in Command Log
- If inoperable/malfunction: notify **ITB Service Desk** and make Telephone Record entry
- If misuse or damage: take immediate action AND notify **ICO AND C.O.**

### Patrol Supervisor — Responsibilities

- Check RMP computers **frequently**
- If misuse/damage: take immediate action AND notify **ICO AND C.O.**
- Record notifications in digital Activity Log

> **Exam Alert:** For computer misuse or damage, both the **Desk Officer** and **Patrol Supervisor** must notify **ICO AND C.O.** — not just one or the other. *(P.G. 219-14)*

### Integrity Control Officer (ICO) Responsibilities

| Duty | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Monthly audit | Conduct monthly audit of Domain Awareness System by **randomly picking 5 MOS** with access |
| Password management | Issue passwords for OLBS, BADS, and LAPS through Omniform; establish and maintain list of ALL passwords issued |
| Authorized users | Establish number of necessary authorized users; ensure users understand security guidelines; maintain sign-on tables |
| Monitor/observe | Frequently observe computer areas/RMPs for compliance; investigate indications of abuse |
| Document | Prepare Report on Typed Letterhead of audit results; Report (49) **filed at command** |

### Key Rules

- Department reserves the right to **review, intercept, access, and disclose ALL** material on and use of Department computer, including **EMAIL**, with or **without notice** to member concerned
- Members have **no expectation of privacy** on Department computer systems
- Unauthorized access or use subject to disciplinary action

> **Exam Alert:** The ICO must conduct a **monthly DAS audit of 5 random members**. The audit report (49) is **filed at command** — it goes nowhere else. *(P.G. 219-14)*

> **Exam Alert:** The Department may review, intercept, and disclose computer activity **with or without notice** — this includes email. *(P.G. 219-14)*

> **Sergeant Focus:** Department reserves the right to review **all** computer activity — members have no privacy expectation on Department systems.

### Focused Audit

- ICO or C.O. can request a **focused audit** of computer activity performed during a particular time period
- Request via 49 to Deputy Commissioner of IT
- Audits may include: MOS password, Tax #, time signed on/off, license checks, etc.

---

## P.G. 219-15: Portable Radios

**Date Effective:** 12/31/24 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 78 | **Pages:** 4

### Purpose

To provide for the issuance, use, and accountability of portable radios.

### Key Points

- Maintain RADIO ASSIGNMENT LOG at each command
- Radio must be carried in an **authorized carrying case**
- Lost radio = **negligence finding** if not in authorized carrying case at time of loss

### Notifications for Lost/Stolen Radio

| Notify |
|--------|
| Operations Unit |
| Electronics Section |
| Internal Affairs Bureau (IAB) |
| Stolen Property Inquiry Unit |

> **Exam Alert:** If a portable radio is lost and was **not in an authorized carrying case**, the member is deemed **negligent**.

> **Exam Alert:** Four notifications required for lost/stolen radio: **Operations Unit, Electronics Section, IAB, and Stolen Property Inquiry Unit**.

---

## P.G. 219-23: Solar Cellular Callboxes

**Date Effective:** 02/04/15 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure all components of a solar cellular callbox are in place and functioning properly.

### Callbox Components

1. Antenna
2. Solar panel
3. Pole
4. Sign — "Emergency Phone"
5. Callbox
6. Handset and cable (cord)

### Procedure

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Respond as directed by radio dispatcher |
| 2 | Verify all 6 components are in place |
| 3 | Make test call to Communications Section (verify operability, callbox number, location) |
| 4 | Prepare COMPLAINT REPORT for vandalism/damaged/missing components, or MV104AN for vehicle collision damage |

### Notifications

| Time | Contact |
|------|---------|
| Mon–Fri, 0800–1600 | Telecommunications Unit |
| All other times | ITB Wheel |

---

## P.G. 219-24: Cellular Telephones Assigned to Patrol Supervisor

**Date Effective:** 08/01/13 | **Pages:** 1

### Purpose

To ensure proper utilization of and accountability for cellular telephones assigned to patrol supervisors.

### Speed Dial Numbers

| Button | Connects To |
|--------|------------|
| 1 | Telephone switchboard |
| 2 | Desk |
| 3 | Patrol borough |
| 4 | Operations Unit |
| 5 | 911 |

### Key Points

- Phones receive **all incoming calls** but only allow the **5 outgoing calls listed above**
- Desk officer ensures phones are operational each tour (batteries charged, car adapters available)
- Command Log entry: name, telephone number, and assignment of supervisor with phone
- Phones not assigned accounted for each tour with Command Log entry

---

## P.G. 219-31: License Plate Reader Devices

**Date Effective:** 11/16/22 | **Last Revision:** 1.0.112 | **Pages:** 2

### Purpose

To provide for control, use, maintenance, and accountability of License Plate Reader (LPR) devices.

### Definition

**LICENSE PLATE READER (LPR)** — A specialized camera and computer that captures license plate numbers, converts them to text, and compares against a "hot list" (stolen, wanted, suspended registration/insurance, etc.).

### Critical Operational Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| NYSPIN verification | **Absolutely essential** — verify through NYSPIN before any law enforcement action |
| Not connected to NYSPIN | LPR is NOT automatically connected to NYSPIN |
| State of registration | LPR does NOT scan state of registration |
| Partial matches | LPR will activate on partial scan match |
| Hot list currency | Do NOT use if hot list not updated within **24 hours** |

### Procedure

| Step | Role | Action |
|------|------|--------|
| 1 | Member | Inspect LPR for operability at beginning of tour; report to desk officer |
| 2 | Member | Activate LPR; record in Activity Log last hot list update time |
| 4 | Desk Officer | Account for each LPR device at beginning of each tour; record in Command Log |
| 5 | Desk Officer | Enter vehicle LPR assignment on CPR Roll Call System |
| 6 | Desk Officer | Contact ITB Service Desk for LPR repairs |
| 7 | Member (on alarm) | **Verify through NYSPIN** before any law enforcement action |
| 8 | Member | Take law enforcement action (arrest, summons, rotation tow) |

### Restrictions

- LPR used **only** for stolen/wanted files and furtherance of criminal investigations
- Any other use is **strictly prohibited**
- Camera lens cleaned only with glass cleaner applied with soft cloth
- LPR equipment must **not be moved** from mounting location
- Vehicle does not go out of service for LPR repair

> **Exam Alert:** **ALWAYS verify through NYSPIN** before any law enforcement action based on an LPR alarm — the device activates on **partial matches** and is NOT connected to NYSPIN. *(P.G. 219-31)*

> **Exam Alert:** The NYSPIN verification requirement has TWO exceptions: (1) additional reasons for a lawful stop exist (e.g., observed VTL infraction), OR (2) circumstances provide sufficient reasonable suspicion to justify a Terry Stop/Level 3 encounter. In those cases, NYSPIN check may be bypassed. *(P.G. 219-31)*

> **Exam Alert:** An RMP does **NOT** go out of service just because the LPR is down. The vehicle continues patrol. *(P.G. 219-31)*

> **See Also:** P.G. 221-15 — Pursuits: be guided by this procedure when taking enforcement action based on LPR hit.

**Real Time Crime Center:** Can be contacted to conduct a search of past records of license plate numbers searched by an LPR device.

---

## P.G. 219-32: Department Mobile Digital Devices

**Date Effective:** 03/26/25 | **Last Revision:** R.O. 15 | **Pages:** 4

### Definition

**MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE** — Any smartphone, tablet, or portable computer issued by the Department.

### Key Rules for Members

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Official use only | Use Department devices for **official business exclusively** |
| Personal devices | Do NOT use personal devices for official business |
| On-duty personal phone | NOT permitted during tour (except authorized meal or emergency) |
| Carry requirement | Carry Department smartphone while on duty |
| NYPD Phone Sticker | Affix NYPD PHONE STICKER (PD589-090) to phone case |
| No app installation | Do NOT install any applications unless approved by Deputy Commissioner, IT |
| No content deletion | Do NOT delete any content (texts, photos, videos, notes, voicemail) |
| Foreign travel | Request ITB Information Security Desk permission **5 business days** prior |

### Lost/Stolen/Damaged

- Notify supervisor and ITB Telecommunications Unit **immediately**
- Process per P.G. 219-20, "Loss or Theft of Department Property"
- Complete DEPARTMENT MOBILE DIGITAL DEVICE REPAIR/REPLACEMENT/RETURN REQUEST (PD589-163)

### Off-Duty Considerations

- Members may secure Department smartphone at command when off-duty
